David is very sincere in prayer. Observe his faith in prayer; God is my rock, on whom I build my hope. Believers should not rest until they have received some sign that their prayers are heard. He prays that he may not be numbered with the wicked. Save me from being entangled in the snares they have laid for me. Save me from being infected with their sins, and from doing as they do. Lord, never leave me to use such deceptive and treacherous methods for my safety, as they use for my ruin. Believers dread the way of sinners; even the best are aware of the danger of being led astray: we should all pray earnestly to God for his grace to keep us. Those who are careful not to participate with sinners in their sins, have reason to hope that they will not receive their punishments. He speaks of the just judgments of the Lord on those who work wickedness, verse #. This is not the language of passion or revenge. It is a prophecy that there will certainly come a day when God will punish every man who persists in his evil deeds. Sinners shall be held accountable, not only for the harm they have done, but for the harm they intended, and did what they could to accomplish. Disregard for the works of the Lord is the cause of sinners' sin, and becomes the cause of their ruin.

Has God heard our requests? Let us then bless his name. The Lord is my strength, to support me, and carry me through all my service and suffering. The heart that truly believes will in due time greatly rejoice: we are to expect joy and peace in believing. God shall have the praise of it; thus must we express our gratitude. Saints rejoice in others' comfort as well as their own: we receive less benefit from the light of the sun, or from the light of God’s countenance, for others sharing therein. The psalmist concludes with a short, but comprehensive prayer. God’s people are his inheritance, and precious in his eyes. He prays that God would save them; that he would bless them with all good, especially the abundance of his ordinances, which are food to the soul. And direct their actions and overrule their affairs for good. Also, lift them up forever; not only those of that age, but his people in every age to come; lift them up as high as heaven. There, and there only, will saints be lifted up forever, never more to sink or be depressed. Save us, Lord Jesus, from our sins; bless us, you Son of Abraham, with the blessing of righteousness; feed us, you good Shepherd of the sheep, and lift us up forever from the dust, O you who are the Resurrection and the Life.
